identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed and buyer-articulated. Primary pain owner: Puneet Agnihotri (Head of Proc Excellence, also Application Dept per 30 Apr attendees). Pain 1 (UX/complexity): On 22 Apr discovery call, Puneet stated Ariba 'is working' but is 'too complex'; they 'don't need that much complexity' for their size and want a better user experience for a 'smaller nation' (source: Avoma 22 Apr, Puneet Agnihotri; MEDDPICC 2.0 23 Apr). Pain 2 (integration): Ariba–SAP integration is a 'big pain'. Puneet: 'integrations are too complex' and Anand has to act as a mediator between SAP ERP and SAP Ariba, which function like two separate companies despite being under SAP parent (source: Avoma 22 Apr, Puneet Agnihotri quote 1658.65-1673.84s; MEDDPICC 2.0 23 Apr). Pain 3 (scope gaps): Need full digital coverage from request to contract/PO with vendor mgmt, CLM, spend analytics (including ERP-only/OEM spend), invoice portal, and shipments (source: MEDDPICC 2.0 23 Apr). Pain 4 (workarounds): On 30 Apr demo, buyer confirmed bid comparisons rely on Excel exports due to Ariba's inability to normalize freight, discounts, and service hierarchies (source: Avoma 30 Apr notes, 4719.745s). Pain 5 (ECC → S/4HANA migration risk): Buyer beginning SAP ECC → S/4HANA migration; open question whether this requires new integration or just changes (Zycus agreed to investigate) (source: MEDDPICC 2.0 23 Apr blockers, Avoma 30 Apr). Pain is operational and technical (not yet quantified financially), confirmed across multiple buyer voices and calls.

paper_process.narrative: Paper process partially mapped. Contracting mechanics: MEDDPICC 2.0 (23 Apr) notes Anand will manage the project and CEO/CPO will act on his recommendation, implying signature authority resides at C-level. No explicit mention of MSA, SOW, legal review, or InfoSec approval process in calls or CRM. Hosting preference confirmed: Europe, not GCC (source: MEDDPICC 2.0 23 Apr). No SI/partner role identified; deal appears direct. Contract expiration forcing function: Ariba contract ends Apr 2027, buyer intends replacement by end 2026 (source: Next Step 22 Apr, MEDDPICC 2.0 23 Apr). Implementation timeline: phased deployment starting Nov-Dec 2026, incremental functionality over six months (source: Avoma 22 Apr notes). Integration scoping confirmed: PR import, master-data sync (materials, GL, cost centers), and ECC → S/4HANA migration support required (source: Avoma 30 Apr notes, MEDDPICC 2.0 23 Apr). Gap: no visibility into legal review, redlining process, procurement approval authority, or InfoSec requirements. Recommended move: after shortlist confirmation, request procurement and legal process overview and typical contract terms to de-risk contracting phase.

economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is confirmed as CEO and CPO (final approval authority), per MEDDPICC 2.0 (23 Apr): 'Final approval must go to CEO and CPO; they will act based on Anand's recommendation.' Budget owner: current Ariba budget will be diverted to Zycus; incremental setup cost requires separate approval (source: MEDDPICC 2.0 23 Apr). However, neither CEO nor CPO has been engaged directly; no calls, no contact roles, no named individuals. The 22 Apr discovery call named the 'Director IT Systems (Main decision maker)' and Puneet Agnihotri (Head of Proc Excellence) as the working-level decision drivers, and MEDDPICC 2.0 states Anand will 'arrange a meeting where you present to CEO and CPO in Kuwait to give a push' after procurement recommends Zycus. Status: EB is identified and mapped at title level, but passive (not yet engaged). Risk: heading into pricing gate (due end-June per 15 Jun call) with no direct EB validation of budget authority, pricing tolerance, or commercial constraints. Recommended move: secure Anand-led CEO/CPO executive briefing before indicative pricing submission.

sources: Avoma call sequence 22 Apr, 30 Apr, 15 Jun 2026
decision_process.narrative: Decision process confirmed via MEDDPICC 2.0 (23 Apr) and call sequence. Steps: (1) One or two detailed demos with procurement manager and 1-3 team members (completed 30 Apr and 15 Jun). (2) Internal alignment between IT and Business teams, then shortlist decision by end-June 2026 (committed 15 Jun call). (3) Request indicative pricing post-shortlist (due after end-June). (4) Reference call. (5) Negotiation. (6) After procurement recommends Zycus, Anand will arrange a meeting where Zycus presents to CEO and CPO in Kuwait 'to give a push.' Parallel: Zycus to assist Anand in building business case/ROI to support approvals (source: MEDDPICC 2.0 23 Apr). Approval chain: CEO and CPO final sign-off, acting on Anand's recommendation; Anand will not push Zycus if procurement doesn't like it (procurement acceptance is mandatory gate). Call sequence 22 Apr → 30 Apr → 15 Jun shows progressive engagement: discovery → core demo → deeper functional validation. Five-vendor RFP field (Ariba incumbent, Coupa, GEP, iValua, JAGGAER per SF Competitors__c) suggests competitive shortlist evaluation underway. Process is buyer-controlled, methodical, and transparent; Zycus is tracking the committed gates.

decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria confirmed across 22 Apr discovery call, 30 Apr demo, and MEDDPICC 2.0 (23 Apr). Core requirements: replace Ariba with a tool that is less complex and more user-friendly, covering sourcing (incl. auctions), contract management and authoring, vendor onboarding/management, supplier risk and performance, invoice portal, shipment/ASN, and spend analytics including ERP-only/OEM spend. Strong SAP integration: connect to ECC now and adapt to S/4HANA during 2-year migration. Modular roadmap: baseline first (S2C + Invoicing likely), then intake (Teams), catalogs, and AI/agentic automation later; they don't want to 'implement everything and not use it' (source: MEDDPICC 2.0 23 Apr). Hosting preference: Europe, not GCC. Evaluation process: detailed demos (core scope + optional/future), procurement team acceptance mandatory, then CEO/CPO presentation. On 30 Apr demo, buyer validated Merlin Intake (Teams front-door), bid normalization to eliminate Excel workarounds, and agentic AI for tactical sourcing as high-interest optional capabilities (source: Avoma 30 Apr notes). On 15 Jun, buyer requested functional clarifications on PO creation and invoice management for direct materials sourcing, and contract-based catalog sourcing (source: Next Step 15 Jun). Criteria are well-documented and buyer-confirmed; fitment is strong on UX, integration, and modularity.

flags: Map the Economic Buyer and secure EB introduction before pricing is submitted. A $200k multi-year platform replacement will require CFO or C-level sign-off; Puneet (Decision Maker) has not named the budget authority despite confirming budget is in process. Ask Puneet directly who will approve the commercial and request an intro this week, before the shortlist decision (due end Jun). Without EB visibility we are flying blind into pricing and negotiation.

Multi-thread beyond Puneet to the Director IT Systems, Dominic Xavier (Head of Procurement), and any CFO/GM before pricing. The Director IT Systems was co-decision maker on the 22 Apr discovery call but did not speak on the 30 Apr demo and is not logged as a contact role (red flag: cooling or excluded?). Dominic Xavier (Head of Procurement, present 30 Apr) is the functional buyer and should be a key influencer but is also not logged. Single-threading through Puneet in a complex enterprise S2P replacement is high-risk; secure direct relationships with IT Systems and Dominic this week to validate the shortlist decision and ensure our demo resonated with both sides of the house.

Co-develop a quantified ROI model with Puneet and Dominic to anchor the business case ahead of shortlist decision. The buyer has articulated clear operational pain (Ariba complexity, manual Excel workarounds, integration pain) but no financial impact. Work with them to quantify: cost of manual bid normalization (FTE hours, error rate), cycle-time savings from PR/RFQ auto-conversion, S/4HANA integration cost avoidance vs Ariba, and headcount productivity gain from the relevant stakeholder automation. A strong ROI story will differentiate us in the shortlist and make the pricing envelope defensible to the EB.

AI/Intake positioned as 'later phases' instead of Phase 1 centerpiece. Buyer stated 'AI and Intake elements will be in later phases due to limited team capabilities' (22 Apr). We are positioning Merlin AI/Intake as optional/future rather than core baseline, risking that the buyer down-selects on price alone and defers the AI differentiation that is our wedge. Risk: we win Phase 1 on commodity S2P fit and lose margin, or we lose the shortlist to a cheaper peer because we did not make AI the centerpiece.

We are positioning Merlin AI/Intake as optional/future rather than core baseline, risking that the buyer down-selects on price alone and defers the AI differentiation that is our wedge. Risk: we win Phase 1 on commodity S2P fit and lose margin, or we lose the shortlist to a cheaper peer because we did not make AI the centerpiece.

Map the Economic Buyer and secure EB introduction before pricing is submitted. A $200k multi-year platform replacement will require CFO or C-level sign-off; Puneet (Decision Maker) has not named the budget authority despite confirming budget is in process. Ask Puneet directly who will approve the commercial and request an intro this week, before the shortlist decision (due end Jun). Without EB visibility we are flying blind into pricing and negotiation.

Request a competitive debrief from Puneet before end-Jun shortlist decision. We are in a five-vendor bake-off (Coupa, GEP, iValua, JAGGAER, plus displaced incumbent Ariba) with zero buyer intelligence on who is leading, how criteria are weighted, or how we are scoring vs peers. Ask Puneet: who is the front-runner, what are the buyer's concerns with each vendor, what is the evaluation weighting (price vs fit vs SAP integration vs AI), and how did our 15 Jun demo compare. Use this to sharpen our pricing positioning and counter any competitor's wedge before the shortlist is finalized.

Multi-thread beyond Puneet to the Director IT Systems, Dominic Xavier (Head of Procurement), and any CFO/GM before pricing. The Director IT Systems was co-decision maker on the 22 Apr discovery call but did not speak on the 30 Apr demo and is not logged as a contact role (red flag: cooling or excluded?). Dominic Xavier (Head of Procurement, present 30 Apr) is the functional buyer and should be a key influencer but is also not logged. Single-threading through Puneet in a complex enterprise S2P replacement is high-risk; secure direct relationships with IT Systems and Dominic this week to validate the shortlist decision and ensure our demo resonated with both sides of the house.

Co-develop a quantified ROI model with Puneet and Dominic to anchor the business case ahead of shortlist decision. The buyer has articulated clear operational pain (Ariba complexity, manual Excel workarounds, integration pain) but no financial impact. Work with them to quantify: cost of manual bid normalization (FTE hours, error rate), cycle-time savings from PR/RFQ auto-conversion, S/4HANA integration cost avoidance vs Ariba, and headcount productivity gain from the relevant stakeholder automation. A strong ROI story will differentiate us in the shortlist and make the pricing envelope defensible to the EB.

value.detail: Buyer stated 'AI and Intake elements will be in later phases due to limited team capabilities' (22 Apr). Risk: we win Phase 1 on commodity S2P fit and lose margin, or we lose the shortlist to a cheaper peer because we did not make AI the centerpiece.

value.flag: Co-develop a quantified ROI model with Puneet and Dominic to anchor the business case ahead of shortlist decision. The buyer has articulated clear operational pain (Ariba complexity, manual Excel workarounds, integration pain) but no financial impact. Work with them to quantify: cost of manual bid normalization (FTE hours, error rate), cycle-time savings from PR/RFQ auto-conversion, S/4HANA integration cost avoidance vs Ariba, and headcount productivity gain from the relevant stakeholder automation. A strong ROI story will differentiate us in the shortlist and make the pricing envelope defensible to the EB.

Situation

  • They use SAP Ariba for S2P, find it too complex and not user‑friendly for a “smaller nation”; integration with SAP is painful despite both being SAP products. They’re beginning an SAP ECC → S/4HANA migration.\xa0Ariba contract ends April 27 - intention is to have a replacement by end of 26.

Impact

  • Usability: They “don’t need that much complexity” but are stuck with it.\xa0
  • Integration: Ariba–SAP integration is “too complex” and they have to act as mediator between two SAP entities.\xa0

(Proposed) Resolution with Zycus

  • Replace Ariba with a simpler, user‑friendly, SAP‑integrated Zycus S2P platform covering procurement through to contracts, vendor, invoices, and shipments; start with a baseline phase (likely S2C+ Invoicing), then add advanced/AI capabilities on a roadmap/later phases.will push Intake considering their priority of a step change in UI/UX.
